open_gaps: The exact immutable statement is not resolved because the domains of n and k are not explicit; all substantive conclusions are conditional on the adopted integer interpretation.
Neither LOW_HIGH_EXCLUSION nor its preliminary three-layer intersection problem is discharged.
No admissible four-interior collision is constructed or globally excluded.
No mechanism establishes exact interior multiplicity q for every nonnegative q.
The terminal negative branch NEGATIVE_T5 and terminal positive branch POSITIVE_SCALING are both incomplete.
The claimed finite decidability of every bounded displacement-word search lacks a proved bound on the variables n and k or another complete decision procedure.
counterexample_searches: Checked the collision-slope orientation against the 3003 chain. Its row-drop/column-rise ratios are 63/3=21 and 1/1=1, so d_i/e_i decreases and e_i/d_i increases, consistent with the substantive content of C11.

Enumerated admissible fibers through row 500. The observed repeated interior structures included the known 3003 chain and supplied no counterexample to C12, although this finite search is not a proof.
Verified the Pell recurrence in C13 directly: it sends (X,Y)=(29,13) to (199,89), yielding (n,k)=(104,39), and then to the stated (714,272) block.

Independently reproduced C16 with exact integer arithmetic. The enumeration gives 3,014,786 high-layer occurrences, 3,014,784 distinct values, and exactly the two stated repeated values: 3003 and 61218182743304701891431482520.
The independent computation found maximum interior multiplicity 3 below 10^30, attained by 3003, confirming the finite conclusion M(a)<=4 on that range.
C13 itself defeats the inference used at the end of C10: fixing a bounded displacement pattern does not bound n and k, since the fixed one-edge pattern d=e=1 has infinitely many solutions.

material_errors.error: The exact cancellation equation is correct, but the conclusion that bounded displacement words are therefore decidable by finite exact search does not follow. A finite polynomial Diophantine system may have unbounded solutions, and no search bound or complete decision algorithm is supplied. C13 demonstrates that even a fixed displacement pattern can generate infinitely many solutions.

The validated DAG encodes NEGATIVE_T5 and POSITIVE_SCALING as simultaneous ordinary dependencies of GOAL, while the claim text requires an alternative: DOMAIN and either NEGATIVE_T5 or POSITIVE_SCALING. The graph therefore does not faithfully encode its own terminal decision rule.
The graph lists both WORD_SCALING and PELL_SCALING as dependencies, although the claim requires success of at least one construction branch. This is another unrepresented OR-dependency.
NORMALIZATION is marked dependency-free even though it is explicitly conditional on resolving or adopting the discrete domain identified by DOMAIN.

The graph omits a node establishing the adjacent-layer Pell family on which PELL_DOUBLE_LIFT relies. The candidate later proves such a family as C13, but the validated subgoal graph does not record that load-bearing dependency.
The candidate expressly leaves every terminal route incomplete and therefore does not prove or disprove the immutable universally quantified statement as required by the acceptance criteria.

notes:
C1-C9 and C11-C16 survive the mathematical checks performed, with C16 independently reproduced rather than accepted from its reported output. C10 contains a genuine logical overreach, and the supplied subgoal DAG has several structural dependency defects, especially failure to encode alternative branches. The candidate is a useful conditional structural report, but it cannot pass the stated acceptance criteria because no terminal proof or disproof is discharged. Source reviewed:
